USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False

LR_DECAY_FACTOR = 0.1
LR_DECAY_EPOCHS = [60, 80]","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"build_scheduler","new":"","old":"","op":"replace_function","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 20)
    cosine_steps = max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps)
    warmup = optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(optimizer, start_factor=0.1, total_iters=warmup_steps)
    cosine = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=cosine_steps, eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.05)
    return optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(optimizer, schedulers=[warmup, cosine], milestones=[warmup_steps])","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"# --- Batch / data hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
BATCH_SIZE = 128
NUM_WORKERS = 0","old":"# --- Batch / data hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
BATCH_SIZE = 64
NUM_WORKERS = 0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Use the fixed step budget more effectively by switching to AdamW, enlarging the batch, and adding a warmup-plus-cosine schedule aligned to the actual step limit; the MLP also gets a modest capacity increase so it can fit CIFAR-10 faster without becoming unwieldy.","secondary_modes":["indexing","layout","topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["micro","topk","indexing"],"rationale":"Push the fixed-step regime harder by giving each step more data and using a more aggressive one-cycle schedule; the current model is already learning, so the best next gain is likely from better budget allocation rather than another architecture jump.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- Optimizer hyperparameters ----------------------------------------------
OPTIMIZER = "adamw"
LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3

MOMENTUM = 0.9

WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4

ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- Batch / data hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
BATCH_SIZE = 128
NUM_WORKERS = 0","new":"# --- Batch / data hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
BATCH_SIZE = 256
NUM_WORKERS = 0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_function","function":"build_scheduler","old":"","new":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=total_steps,
        pct_start=0.15,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=50.0,
    )"}]}
